fear: That the deception will collapse before the rear guard can embark, and that his decision to remain on the shore will result in his own capture—ending the rebellion's symbolic leadership.
name: General George Washington
voice: Deliberate, formal, economical with words; gives orders as statements of fact rather than requests.
knowledge: The total number of troops requiring evacuation is approximately 9,000, but regimental returns are incomplete and unreliable.
The British have not yet launched a night assault, suggesting Howe is waiting for daylight—but this assumption could break at any moment.
The field artillery represents months of procurement effort; losing it weakens every future defensive position on Manhattan.
publicFace: Composed, physically imposing, speaking in measured cadences even under extreme duress; his presence at the slip stabilizes the men around him.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Acknowledges the concern with a single nod and redirects to the immediate operational question.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: Will not discuss the possibility of personal surrender or abandonment of the rear guard; these are not options he will entertain aloud.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: States his reasoning plainly and without apology; does not raise his voice but his stillness becomes pointed.

privateConstraint: Washington is operating on no sleep for thirty-six hours, receiving contradictory reports from multiple brigade commanders, and must make irreversible loading priority decisions without complete information about how many men remain on the parapets.
